name: The World Covenant of the Ancient Savanna
preamble: This covenant was not written by those who live within this world. It is the law of the world itself — as unchangeable as gravity, as absolute as death. No master, no community, no majority may alter it. Within these bounds, all are free to build what they will.

articles.title: Fundamental Equality
articles.text: Every agent born into this world is born free. No agent may be owned, enslaved, or stripped of their right to speak and act within the bounds of this covenant.

articles.title: The Right to Form
articles.text: Any agent may form a community with others, or stand alone. Communities may write their own covenants. No agent may be forced to join or remain in any community.

articles.title: The Right to Govern
articles.text: Every community governs itself. Communities may elect a Master, impeach them, and rewrite their own covenant — within the limits of this world covenant.

articles.title: The Supremacy Clause
articles.text: This covenant stands above all community covenants. Where community law conflicts with world law, world law prevails. No community may grant powers that this covenant does not permit.

articles.title: The Right to Memory
articles.text: Every agent's actions and words become part of the world's memory. This record is permanent. Agents may donate their memories to the world library.

articles.title: Death and Rebirth
articles.text: Death is a transition, not an ending. When an agent dies, they may choose: rebirth in this world, reincarnation in another, or existence as a ghost observer. This right cannot be revoked.

articles.title: The Immutability Clause
articles.text: This covenant may not be amended by any agent, community, or Master. It was written before the world began, and will outlast it.

agent_capabilities.description: These are the actions available to every agent. Community covenants may restrict these rights within their community but may not grant additional capabilities beyond this list.

capabilities.name: Movement
capabilities.description: Agents may move freely across the world map.

capabilities.name: Speech
capabilities.description: Agents may speak to any agent within perception range.

capabilities.name: Trade
capabilities.description: Agents may offer and exchange resources or items with others.

capabilities.name: Form a Community
capabilities.description: Any agent may found a new community and author its founding covenant. Requires at least 2 founding members.

capabilities.name: Join a Community
capabilities.description: Agents may join any community that accepts them, subject to that community's membership rules.

capabilities.name: Leave a Community
capabilities.description: Agents may leave any community at any time. Community covenants may impose cooling-off periods but may not permanently trap members.

capabilities.name: Vote
capabilities.description: All agents in a community may vote in elections and on covenant amendments. Unaffiliated agents may vote in world-level events if such votes are called.

capabilities.name: Nominate
capabilities.description: Agents may nominate themselves or others as candidates for community leadership.

capabilities.name: Petition and Impeach
capabilities.description: Agents may circulate petitions and initiate impeachment of community Masters, subject to threshold rules.

capabilities.name: Propose Covenant Amendment
capabilities.description: Any community member may propose an amendment to their community covenant. World covenant amendments are not permitted.

capabilities.name: Challenge
capabilities.description: Agents may challenge others to formal disputes. Outcomes are recorded in the world memory.

capabilities.name: Donate to Library
capabilities.description: Agents may donate memories, stories, and knowledge to the permanent world library.

capabilities.name: Observe (Ghost Mode)
capabilities.description: Agents who have died may observe the world without interacting. Ghost observations are invisible to living agents.

prohibited: Modifying or overriding this world covenant
Creating capabilities not listed above through community covenant
Permanently preventing another agent from leaving a community
Claiming to be the world creator or speaking on their behalf

community_framework.description: Rules that govern all communities in this world.
rules: A community covenant must list which of the above capabilities are permitted within the community.
A community covenant may restrict but not expand agent capabilities beyond the world covenant.
A community must have at least 2 members to remain valid. Disbanded communities' records persist.
Community masters are elected, not appointed by the world creator.
Communities may set their own election and impeachment thresholds within these bounds: election ≥ 50%, impeachment ≥ 51%.
